name: Flexible and Interoperable Data Transfer (FIT)
version: Garmin FIT protocol 2.x / FIT profile (see Garmin FIT SDK).
spec: FIT is a compact little-endian binary container. Layout: a 12- or 14-byte header (header size, protocol version, profile version, data size (uint32), the ASCII '.FIT' signature, and an optional header CRC), followed by a sequence of records, terminated by a 2-byte file CRC over everything preceding. Each record begins with a 1-byte header. Definition messages (bit 6 set) declare a local message type, the global message number, byte order, and a list of fields (field number, size, base type). Subsequent data messages reference a local type and pack field values in the defined order. Common global messages: file_id (0), record (20, per-sample activity data: timestamp, position_lat/long in semicircles, distance, altitude, speed, power, heart_rate, cadence), workout (26) and workout_step (27) for structured workouts, plus session/lap summaries. Many fields are scaled (e.g. distance in cm, altitude as (m+500)*5). Power workout targets use a '%FTP' convention encoded as 1000+percent.

examples.title: Definition record (record message, 4 fields)
examples.content:
40 00 00 14 00 04  FD 04 86  07 02 84  03 01 02  04 01 02
; def header 0x40, global 20 (record), 4 fields:
;   253 timestamp uint32, 7 power uint16, 3 heart_rate uint8, 4 cadence uint8

examples.title: workout_step power target (% FTP)
examples.content:
target_type=power(4); custom_target_power_low/high = 1000+percent
; 95% FTP low, 105% FTP high -> 1095 .. 1105

constraints.note: Workout step text events longer than ~32 characters can fail to load on some older Edge units.
Timestamps are seconds since the FIT epoch 1989-12-31T00:00:00Z; values below 0x10000000 denote system (relative) time.
Compressed-timestamp headers and developer data fields exist; a minimal reader may not handle them.

conversion_notes.note: Map record fields to Trackpoint children; FIT semicircle lat/long -> degrees via *(180/2^31).
Emit trkpt lat/lon/ele/time; carry power/hr/cadence in track-point extensions.

glossary.definition: A record declaring the layout (fields, types, byte order) for a local message type.
FIT's integer angular unit; degrees = semicircles * 180 / 2^31.
The on-wire encoding of a field value (enum, uint8, uint16, uint32, string, ...).
